name: Dilled Chicken Pot Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Green peas, cooked (cut into 1-inch pieces)
3 tablespoons Butter or vegetable shortening (or a combination), cold
recipeInstructions:
1. For the Filling: Melt butter over low heat in a large saucepan. Add celery, onion, and bay leaf; cover and cook about 10 minutes, or until vegetables are soft.
2. Sprinkle flour over the vegetables; stir to incorporate. Cook for about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in chicken stock and milk or cream. Cook sauce over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove bay leaf and add Tabasco, salt, and pepper.
4. Stir in chicken, potatoes, carrots, peas/beans, and dill. Keep filling warm while making biscuit dough.
5. For the Biscuits: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
6. Sift flour, baking powder, and salt into a bowl. Make a well; cut fat into dry ingredients. Add milk and dill; stir with fork until mixture leaves sides of bowl.
7. Transfer chicken and vegetable mixture to an ovenproof casserole dish. Drop biscuit dough over filling using a large spoon; space evenly to make six biscuits.
8. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until biscuits are lightly browned and pie is bubbling. Serve hot.
